item.title: Samson: A Tyndalston Story coming to PS5, Xbox Series in September
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Xbox Series

Currently available for PC.

Liquid Swords will release “consequence-heavy noir action game” Samson: A Tyndalston Story for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series in September, the developer told The Games Business.

Samson: A Tyndalston Story first launched for PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on April 8.

Here is an overview of the game, via Liquid Swords:

About

Samson: A Tyndalston Story is a pressure-driven action experience built around debt, consequences, and survival in one of the most unforgiving cities imaginable.

Players take on the role of Samson McCray, a man fighting to stay ahead of a crushing debt that tightens by the hour. Violence, desperation, and quick judgment are the only tools he has left as Tyndalston pushes back at every turn. Combat is grounded, physical, and dangerous. The city responds to your actions, forcing you into a loop where pressure defines every choice you make.

Samson: A Tyndalston Story is built on a simple, brutal truth: every day costs you. Debt grows with interest, and time works against you. Each job burns a limited pool of Action Points and every decision shifts how the city treats you—there are no do-overs. You move forward because standing still makes everything worse.

Founded by Christofer Sundberg, Liquid Swords brings together key developers whose experience shaped the Just Cause series and Mad Max. The team’s background in physical action, systemic design, and character-focused worlds forms the backbone of Samson: A Tyndalston Story. This is a sharp, contained story built to hit hard from the opening frame.

Key Features

  • Debt Defines Everything – Your debt grows every day. Your next move determines whether you stay afloat or collapse under the weight.
  • Action Points Set the Pace – Every job drains a limited daily pool. Every choice forces a
    trade-off.
  • Combat With Consequence – Fights are grounded and punishing. Violence is a tool, not a spectacle.
  • A City That Pushes Back – Tyndalston tracks your decisions. Faces, factions, and streets shift with your actions. Nothing is forgiven.


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/samson-a-tyndalston-story-coming-to-ps5-xbox-series-in-september
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sydless launches May 27
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Bouncing ball fast-paced first-person shooter dated.

Sydless, a fast-paced first-person shooter where your only weapon is a deadly bouncing ball, will launch for PC via Steam on May 27, developer Opia Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

Sydless is a fast-paced first-person shooter built around momentum, skill, and a deadly bouncing ball, the more you punch it, the faster it gets—turning it into a high-speed, projectile of destruction. It fuses together classic movement shooters with a unique bouncing ball weapon.

Reality is breaking. Trapped in an anomaly, you are Diego, an employee of Rundy’s Burger, a fast-food chain at the end of the ‘90s. But something is wrong. The restaurant keeps shifting, twisting into increasingly distorted versions of itself.

Use your agility and mastery of movement to navigate the chaos and avoid relentless attacks from the customers, twisted by the shifting reality of the restaurant. Destroy them with your only weapon—a high-speed bouncing ball that gains more power the faster it moves. Punch it, control it and Turn every room into a deadly playground.

Progress through four chapters, each unlocking new powers to push your abilities even further. Every level features a ranking system based on speed and skill.

Master each stage, and push your skills to the limit as you fight to escape the loop… if escape is even possible.

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/sydless-launches-may-27
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
